meta.subtitle: Iran War File — Daily Assessment
meta.contextNote: Cycle covers the 24-hour period ending 0600 UTC 07 March 2026. All times UTC unless noted.

strategicHeader.headlineJudgment: We assess with high confidence that the conflict has entered a critical multi-theatre escalation phase — Iran launched its first direct ballistic missile strike on Israeli soil at Haifa, suspended IAEA inspector access at Fordow, and escalated drone attacks across Gulf states, while Israel expanded operations to include Tehran's Mehrabad International Airport and an IDF ground incursion into Lebanon.
strategicHeader.trajectoryRationale: Simultaneous horizontal escalation across military (Haifa strike, Lebanon ground ops), nuclear (Fordow IAEA suspension), economic (Brent $147/bbl, IEA emergency release), and maritime (Houthi Red Sea resumption, UNIFIL casualties) domains with no diplomatic off-ramp.

flashPoints.headline: Iran breaches deterrence threshold with direct Haifa strike
flashPoints.detail: We assess this constitutes a deliberate escalation threshold breach — 18 IRGC Shahab-3 missiles targeted Haifa port district at 0218 UTC; four penetrated Iron Dome/Arrow-3 defenses, damaging fuel storage and wounding seven dock workers. Iran simultaneously launched missiles over Tel Aviv prompting sirens and interceptor launches.

flashPoints.headline: Iran suspends IAEA access at Fordow, obscuring breakout timeline
flashPoints.detail: We assess with high confidence that the Fordow suspension removes international visibility into Iran's highest-enrichment activities — continuous monitoring equipment disconnected; IAEA Director General declared 'serious breach' of NPT safeguards; ICG estimates Iran has sufficient 60% UF6 for one weapon's worth of HEU in 10–14 days if political decision is made.

flashPoints.headline: Brent breaches $147 as IEA activates third emergency reserve release
flashPoints.detail: We judge it highly likely that markets are pricing sustained conflict — Brent at $147.20/bbl with Hormuz throughput at 67% of normal (14M vs 21M bpd); IEA activated 60M barrel release over 30 days, the third emergency activation in 12 months; Asian LNG spot surged 40% to $32/MMBtu.

executive.bluf: We assess with high confidence that the conflict has crossed into a critical multi-theatre escalation phase with no near-term de-escalation pathway. Iran's simultaneous first direct ballistic missile strike on Israeli soil and suspension of IAEA access at Fordow represents a deliberate shift from calibrated response to horizontal escalation — Tehran is now pursuing pressure across military, nuclear, and economic domains simultaneously. The energy system confirms the trajectory: Brent at $147.20/bbl, Hormuz throughput at 67% of normal, and LNG spot prices surging 40% represent structural supply constraints that emergency reserves cannot offset. Canada faces direct exposure through NATO alliance commitments, LNG import partner vulnerability (Japan, South Korea), and Prime Minister Carney's concurrent defense-cooperation engagement in Tokyo.

keyJudgments.text: We judge it highly likely that Iran has escalated to direct strikes on Israeli territory while coordinating multi-front pressure — the Haifa port strike, Tel Aviv missile launches, and concurrent Houthi Red Sea operations indicate resistance-axis coordination rather than isolated Iranian action.
keyJudgments.basis: First confirmed IRGC ballistic missile strike on Israeli soil at Haifa; Iranian missiles over Tel Aviv; USS Gravely intercepted four Houthi ASBMs in Red Sea; MV Nordic Hawk struck by UAV at Bab el-Mandeb — all within 8-hour window.

keyJudgments.text: We assess with high confidence that further escalation is almost certain — Iran's Fordow IAEA suspension combined with Trump's demand for 'unconditional surrender' eliminates both monitoring transparency and negotiated settlement as de-escalation mechanisms.
keyJudgments.basis: IAEA Director General declared 'serious breach' of NPT; Trump stated no deal until 'unconditional surrender'; Rubio told Arab states war to last 'several more weeks'; UNSC ceasefire resolution vetoed by Russia-China.

keyJudgments.text: We judge it highly likely that energy markets have entered a sustained crisis phase — Brent at $147.20/bbl, Hormuz at 67% throughput, IEA third emergency release in 12 months, and drone attacks on Iraqi and UAE oil infrastructure represent compounding supply constraints.
keyJudgments.basis: CENTCOM confirms daylight-only Hormuz escort transits at 14M bpd vs 21M normal; IEA released 60M barrels; drone attacks on Basra oil facility and Fujairah UAE facility confirmed; Asian LNG +40%.

keyJudgments.text: Available evidence suggests the diplomatic track has stalled — the Russia-China UNSC veto, Oman's characterization of back-channel talks as 'not productive', and Trump's demand for unconditional surrender indicate no near-term ceasefire pathway exists through any available channel.
keyJudgments.basis: UNSC ceasefire vetoed by Russia-China; Omani FM briefed EU that Muscat channel 'active but not productive'; Iran conditioning ceasefire on complete secondary sanctions lift; Trump demands unconditional surrender.

keyJudgments.text: Reporting indicates, though corroboration is limited, that Iran's internet disruption has partially recovered to 40% connectivity — whether this reflects infrastructure repair, government calibration, or external operational decisions remains undetermined.
keyJudgments.basis: NetBlocks confirms partial recovery from 1% to 40% connectivity; CISA Advisory AA26-063A attributes active OT exploitation to IRGC-affiliated 'Volt Sparrow'; Recorded Future identifies new APT35 C2 infrastructure targeting Israeli banking.

domains.title: BATTLESPACE · KINETIC
domains.assessmentQuestion: What is the current disposition of forces, and what military operations occurred across all theatres in the last 24 hours?

keyJudgment.text: We judge it highly likely the conflict has expanded to three simultaneous fronts — Iran theatre, Lebanon ground operations, and resumed Houthi Red Sea attacks — with coordinated resistance-axis timing indicating strategic rather than opportunistic escalation.
keyJudgment.basis: IRGC Haifa strike, Houthi Red Sea interdiction, and Hezbollah probing operations occurred within 8-hour window; IDF expanding Lebanon incursion with full-scale invasion preparations visible; Israeli strikes now targeting Tehran Mehrabad Airport area.

bodyParagraphs.text: As of 0200 UTC 07 Mar, the Israeli military launched a 'broad wave of strikes' at Tehran including impacts near Mehrabad International Airport, a busy civilian airfield; state media footage showed multiple explosions and columns of smoke across the capital (NYT, 07 Mar 0200 UTC). The IRGC launched approximately 18 Shahab-3 variant ballistic missiles at the Haifa port district at 0218 UTC; Iron Dome and Arrow-3 intercepted 14 of 18, with four impacting the industrial waterfront, damaging fuel storage and wounding seven dock workers (CTP-ISW Evening, 07 Mar). Iranian missiles were also observed over Tel Aviv, prompting sirens and interceptor launches (Al Jazeera, 07 Mar 0111 UTC). The IDF confirmed destruction of Khamenei's bunker, which was being used by top military brass; Trump demanded 'unconditional surrender' from Iran, with Rubio telling Arab states the war would last 'several more weeks' (Times of Israel, 06 Mar 1902 UTC). Iran's drone attacks on Gulf states continued, with an 11-year-old Iranian girl killed in Kuwait by shrapnel from an Iranian drone (Times of Israel, 07 Mar 0124 UTC).

As of 0200 UTC 07 Mar, BBC correspondents observed IDF troops and tanks massing near the Lebanese border with speculation mounting of a full-scale ground invasion to fight Hezbollah (BBC, 06 Mar 1616 UTC). Five IDF soldiers were seriously wounded by a Hezbollah rocket, with three more lightly hurt including a minister's son; dozens of IRGC officers reportedly fled the Lebanese capital as the Air Force pounded Beirut's Dahieh suburb (Times of Israel, 06 Mar 1459 UTC). Two UNIFIL peacekeepers from Ghana were critically wounded after their base was struck; the officers' mess 'burnt down completely' with the Lebanese president blaming Israel (Times of Israel, 07 Mar 0151 UTC). In the Red Sea, CENTCOM confirmed USS Gravely intercepted four Houthi anti-ship ballistic missiles at 2347 UTC, with follow-on F/A-18 strikes against Hudaydah launch infrastructure; UKMTO reported MV Nordic Hawk struck by armed UAV at Bab el-Mandeb, with three crew wounded (CENTCOM, 07 Mar; UKMTO WARNO 042/2026).

We assess the IRGC's direct strike on Haifa constitutes a fundamental threshold breach — Iran has abandoned the proxy-mediated retaliation model that constrained escalation through the first week of conflict. The simultaneity of the Haifa strike, Tel Aviv missile launches, and Houthi Red Sea operations indicates coordinated resistance-axis pressure rather than opportunistic Iranian retaliation. Hezbollah's engagement pattern — ATGM strikes followed by withdrawal, characterized by CTP-ISW as 'probing IDF response cadence' — is consistent with reconnaissance-in-force, not offensive preparation. Israel is backing a potential Kurdish offensive into Iran, with sides in talks for a year and Kurdish militants poised to assault border territories (Times of Israel, 06 Mar). The UNIFIL casualties create a new political dimension: peacekeeping force engagement risks broadening international involvement. The NYT reports Iran's drone volleys, while less destructive than ballistic missiles, could deplete Gulf state air defenses through sustained attrition (NYT, 06 Mar 1750 UTC).

domains.title: ESCALATION · TRAJECTORY
domains.assessmentQuestion: What is the probability that the conflict will escalate, de-escalate, or stabilize in the next 24–72 hours?

keyJudgment.text: We assess with high confidence that further escalation is almost certain — Iran's Fordow IAEA suspension combined with direct strikes on Israeli soil and Trump's unconditional surrender demand has eliminated all de-escalation mechanisms simultaneously.
keyJudgment.basis: IAEA declared 'serious breach'; ICG estimates 10–14 day nuclear breakout if political decision made; Trump demands unconditional surrender; UNSC ceasefire vetoed; Omani back-channel 'not productive.'

bodyParagraphs.text: As of 0600 UTC 07 Mar, IAEA Director General Grossi notified the Board of Governors that Iran suspended inspector access to the Fordow Fuel Enrichment Plant; continuous monitoring equipment was disconnected, and Grossi characterized this as a 'serious breach' of NPT safeguards obligations (IAEA, 07 Mar 0600 UTC). ICG estimates Iran possesses sufficient 60%-enriched UF6 to produce one weapon's worth of HEU within 10–14 days if a political decision is made (ICG, 07 Mar). The Pentagon deployed six B-52H Stratofortress bombers to Diego Garcia in what analysts assess is a deliberate signaling measure directed at Tehran (CFR Daily, 07 Mar). The UK Foreign Secretary and French Foreign Minister issued a joint statement demanding Iran restore IAEA access within 48 hours (AP, 07 Mar). The US Senate failed to rein in Trump's war powers on Iran; the measure passed largely along party lines but will not halt military action (BBC, 04 Mar 2342 UTC).

We assess Iran is pursuing deliberate horizontal escalation across military, nuclear, and economic domains simultaneously. The Fordow suspension is a calculated escalation lever rather than a reactive measure — by removing international visibility into enrichment activities, Tehran creates ambiguity about nuclear breakout timeline that serves as both deterrent and negotiating leverage. The convergence of direct military strikes on Israeli territory, nuclear safeguards breach, Gulf state drone campaign, and Red Sea shipping disruption within a 12-hour window indicates strategic coordination across the resistance axis. BBC Persian analysis assesses Iran's strategy centers on endurance and deterrence — a belief Tehran can absorb strikes longer than its adversaries sustain costs (BBC, 05 Mar 1840 UTC). We assess de-escalation pathways are functionally closed: Trump demands unconditional surrender, the UNSC is deadlocked, and Oman's back-channel has produced no progress. The 72-hour escalation probability remains at 95–99%.

domains.title: ENERGY · ECONOMIC
domains.assessmentQuestion: How are energy markets and global supply chains responding to conflict developments?

keyJudgment.text: We judge it highly likely that energy markets have entered a sustained crisis — Brent at $147.20/bbl, Hormuz at 67% throughput, IEA third emergency release, and direct attacks on Iraqi and UAE oil infrastructure represent compounding supply constraints exceeding global mitigation capacity.
keyJudgment.basis: CENTCOM confirms daylight-only Hormuz escort at 14M vs 21M bpd normal; IEA released 60M barrels; drone struck Basra oil facility; Fujairah UAE facility damaged; Asian LNG +40% to $32/MMBtu.

bodyParagraphs.text: As of 0600 UTC 07 Mar, Brent crude opened at $147.20/bbl, the highest since July 2022, continuing the trajectory from $92.69 in the previous cycle (Reuters, 07 Mar). A drone evaded defenses and ignited a massive fire at an oil facility for foreign companies in southern Iraq's Basra province (Al Jazeera, 06 Mar 2344 UTC). Debris from an intercepted drone damaged an oil facility in Fujairah, UAE (NYT, 06 Mar 1750 UTC). CENTCOM confirmed the Strait of Hormuz transit corridor operating under Combined Maritime Forces escort with transits restricted to daylight hours; throughput at approximately 14 million bpd versus the normal 21 million bpd (CENTCOM, 07 Mar). Two VLCCs diverted to the Cape of Good Hope route, adding 14 days to voyage time. The IEA activated emergency reserves for the third time in 12 months, releasing 60 million barrels over 30 days; Saudi Arabia pledged to maximize production within OPEC+ quotas (Reuters, 07 Mar). Asian LNG spot prices surged 40% week-on-week to $32/MMBtu; South Korea and Japan activated strategic LNG reserves; European gas storage at 48% heading into shoulder season (CNBC Energy, 07 Mar).

We assess the direct attacks on Iraqi and UAE oil infrastructure represent a dangerous new vector — Iran's drone campaign is now targeting production facilities in countries that were not direct parties to the initial conflict, creating compounding supply constraints beyond the Hormuz chokepoint. The IEA's third emergency release in 12 months signals strategic reserve depletion is itself becoming a constraint; the 60M barrel release covers approximately 30 days of the 7M bpd Hormuz deficit. Al Jazeera characterizes the Iran war as 'the latest threat to a global economy already rattled by Trump' with rising energy prices threatening to stoke inflation worldwide (Al Jazeera, 07 Mar 0027 UTC). Canadian energy export exposure is limited directly, but LNG import partners Japan and South Korea face acute supply risk with strategic reserves activated. We assess $140+/bbl Brent is sustainable for the conflict duration absent Hormuz reopening.

domains.title: DIPLOMATIC · POLITICAL
domains.assessmentQuestion: What diplomatic initiatives are active, and how are key state actors positioning?

keyJudgment.text: Available evidence suggests the diplomatic track is functionally paralyzed — Trump's demand for unconditional surrender, the Russia-China UNSC veto, and Oman's assessment that back-channel talks are 'not productive' indicate near-term negotiations cannot constrain military operations by either party.
keyJudgment.basis: UNSC ceasefire vetoed; Trump demands unconditional surrender; Omani FM characterizes back-channel as 'not productive'; Iran conditions ceasefire on complete secondary sanctions lift; GCC fractured with UAE maintaining $25B annual Iran trade.

bodyParagraphs.text: As of 0600 UTC 07 Mar, President Trump stated there would be 'no deal with Iran until unconditional surrender' (Al Jazeera, 07 Mar 0000 UTC). Secretary Rubio reportedly told Arab states the war would last 'several more weeks' (Times of Israel, 06 Mar 1902 UTC). The UNSC failed to pass an emergency ceasefire resolution after Russia and China exercised their veto; 11 voted in favour with 3 abstentions (AP, 07 Mar). Saudi Arabia is intensifying talks with Iran, with Gulf state officials complaining they were not given earlier notice about initial US-Israeli strikes (Times of Israel, 06 Mar 2324 UTC). Qatar partially reopened airspace for evacuation and cargo flights, though scheduled services remain suspended (Al Jazeera, 06 Mar 2208 UTC). Azerbaijan announced it foiled Iranian terror attacks targeting a synagogue, the Israeli embassy, Jewish community leaders, and a pipeline servicing Israeli oil imports; at least 7 Azerbaijani nationals were detained (Times of Israel, 06 Mar 2100 UTC). Russia is reportedly supplying Iran with intelligence on US military assets while Ukraine dispatched military drone experts to the Gulf to assist US forces (Times of Israel, 06 Mar 1707 UTC). German Chancellor Merz declared 'we stand with Israel' (Al Jazeera, 07 Mar 0106 UTC). Canadian PM Carney stated he backs strikes on Iran 'with some regret' and said he is 'unable to rule out military participation' in the conflict, characterizing it as 'an extreme example of a rupturing world order'; Carney is in Tokyo signing strategic agreements in defense and energy with Japan amid oil supply concerns (AP, 06 Mar; NYT, 06 Mar 1640 UTC). NATO air defenses intercepted an Iranian ballistic missile headed toward Turkey; Iran denied the missile was intended for Turkish territory; Ankara confirmed the missile was 'not intended for Turkiye' but was intercepted by NATO defenses; Erdogan stated US attacks on Iran are a 'clear violation of international law' while simultaneously 'engaging with all sides to end Iran war' (Reuters, 06 Mar; NYT, 06 Mar). Iranian acting leader Ghalibaf stated Iran's fate 'will not be decided by Epstein's gang' — a reference to Trump administration demands for a role in choosing the next supreme leader (Al Jazeera, 06 Mar 1929 UTC).

We assess the combination of Trump's unconditional surrender demand and Iran's maximalist precondition of complete secondary sanctions relief has created a structurally incompatible negotiating framework — no mediator can bridge this gap within the current conflict dynamics. The Russia-China veto has eliminated the primary multilateral constraint on escalation, and the NYT observes that Russia's global influence is eroding as Trump 'out-Putins Putin' — Moscow's intelligence support to Iran provides tactical assistance but cannot alter the strategic trajectory (NYT, 06 Mar 1139 UTC). The GCC fracture noted by ICG is significant: UAE's $25B annual trade with Iran creates an incentive to resist full coalition alignment with Washington. The NATO interception of an Iranian missile over Turkey is the most significant Article 5-adjacent event this cycle — while Ankara confirmed the missile was not intended for Turkish territory, any future confirmed targeting of a NATO member would trigger collective defense obligations. Carney's statement that Canada cannot rule out military participation represents the first concrete indication of potential Canadian force commitment; combined with his Tokyo defense-cooperation engagement, this signals Canadian strategic recalibration driven by the Iran conflict. Ghalibaf's defiant rhetoric indicates Iranian leadership continuity and rejection of US-imposed succession — the regime is consolidating internally despite Khamenei's death. We assess no diplomatic breakthrough is probable within the 72-hour window.

domains.title: CYBER · INFORMATION OPS
domains.assessmentQuestion: What cyber operations or information warfare activities have been detected?

keyJudgment.text: Reporting indicates, though corroboration is limited, that Iran's internet has partially recovered to 40% while IRGC-affiliated cyber actors escalate offensive operations targeting Western critical infrastructure and Israeli financial systems.
keyJudgment.basis: NetBlocks reports partial connectivity recovery to 40%; CISA Advisory AA26-063A attributes OT exploitation to 'Volt Sparrow'; Recorded Future identifies new APT35 C2 infrastructure.

bodyParagraphs.text: As of 0600 UTC 07 Mar, NetBlocks reported Iran's internet connectivity partially recovered to approximately 40% of normal from the previous 1% — the disruption which began at 0322 UTC continues to affect TIC, MCI, and Rightel providers with social media and VPN traffic suppressed (NetBlocks, 07 Mar). BBC reports Iranians are coping with daily strikes and security crackdowns while trying to maintain contact with family through intermittent connectivity, with checkpoints proliferating across major cities (BBC, 05 Mar 2055 UTC). CISA issued Advisory AA26-063A attributing active exploitation of Unitronics PLC vulnerabilities in water/wastewater and energy sector OT networks to IRGC-affiliated group 'Volt Sparrow'; FBI and CISA recommend immediate patching and network segmentation (CISA, 07 Mar). Recorded Future's Insikt Group identified new APT35 (Charming Kitten) C2 infrastructure targeting Israeli banking and telecommunications with updated HYPERSCRAPE credential-harvesting malware; high confidence attribution to MOIS (Recorded Future, 07 Mar). Symantec reported Iranian Seedworm APT group has infiltrated US infrastructure and defense supply chain networks (Industrial Cyber, 06 Mar). Multiple cybersecurity firms confirmed US Cyber Command conducted cyberattacks ahead of and concurrent with kinetic Iran strikes, corroborating the cyber-kinetic coordination assessed in the previous cycle (Risky Business, 06 Mar).

We assess the partial internet recovery to 40% is ambiguous — it could indicate infrastructure repair, deliberate government recalibration to restore limited economic functions, or external operational adjustments. The IRGC's information warfare claim that all 18 Haifa missiles hit their targets — directly contradicted by Israeli military statements — indicates Tehran is prioritizing domestic morale narratives over credible external communications (Iran International, 07 Mar). The CISA advisory on Volt Sparrow OT exploitation is significant for Canadian critical infrastructure: Unitronics PLCs are deployed in North American water and energy systems, and the advisory recommends immediate defensive action. We assess Iran's cyber response capability remains partially operational despite kinetic degradation, with APT35 infrastructure expansion suggesting pre-positioned offensive operations. The limiting factor on this assessment is the absence of Tier 1 cyber attribution; current indicators rely exclusively on Tier 3 sources.

domains.title: WAR RISK INSURANCE · MARITIME FINANCE
domains.assessmentQuestion: How are insurance markets pricing conflict risk and what are the implications for maritime trade?

keyJudgment.text: Available evidence suggests war risk premiums are pricing sustained conflict with Hormuz restricted to daylight escort-only transit — the VLCC Cape diversions, Houthi resumption of Red Sea attacks, and MV Nordic Hawk strike validate underwriter assessment that dual-chokepoint risk exceeds acceptable thresholds.
keyJudgment.basis: CENTCOM confirms daylight-only Hormuz escort; two VLCCs diverted to Cape route; UKMTO confirms MV Nordic Hawk struck at Bab el-Mandeb; P&I club cancellations from previous cycle remain in effect.

bodyParagraphs.text: As of 0600 UTC 07 Mar, the Strait of Hormuz transit corridor remains under Combined Maritime Forces escort with commercial transits restricted to daylight hours; throughput at approximately 14 million bpd versus the normal 21 million bpd (CENTCOM, 07 Mar). Two VLCC tankers have diverted to the Cape of Good Hope route following insurance market guidance, adding approximately 14 days to voyage time and proportional fuel cost increases. UKMTO reported MV Nordic Hawk (Liberian-flagged, 74,000 DWT bulk carrier) struck by armed UAV at position 12°30'N 043°20'E at 0455 UTC in the Bab el-Mandeb strait; minor superstructure damage with three crew sustaining blast injuries; vessel proceeding to Djibouti (UKMTO WARNO 042/2026, 07 Mar). P&I club war risk cancellations from the previous cycle (Gard, Skuld, NorthStandard, London P&I, American Club) remain in effect for Persian Gulf waters. The Trump administration's $20 billion DFC insurance program announced in the previous cycle requires several weeks to operationalize according to Marsh estimates.

We assess insurance markets are now pricing dual-chokepoint risk — the simultaneous Hormuz restriction and Houthi Red Sea resumption creates a scenario where neither the Gulf nor the Suez/Red Sea alternative offers acceptable risk profiles for underwriters. The VLCC Cape diversions signal that underwriters have concluded both transit corridors exceed acceptable thresholds even with military escort. The MV Nordic Hawk attack at Bab el-Mandeb validates this assessment and will reinforce premium hardening for Red Sea routing. The P&I club cancellations from the previous cycle remain the binding constraint on Gulf transit; the Trump administration's DFC insurance program is weeks from operational status. Canadian-flagged vessel exposure is minimal, but Canadian P&I clubs with Gulf reinsurance exposure may face capital calls, and Canadian commodity importers using Cape routing face structural cost increases of $4–6/MMBtu for LNG and proportional increases for crude cargoes.

warningIndicators.detail: Iran suspended inspector access to Fordow; continuous monitoring disconnected. IAEA Director General declared 'serious breach' of NPT safeguards. ICG estimates 10–14 day nuclear breakout timeline if political decision made.
warningIndicators.assessment: Fordow suspension removes international visibility into enrichment activity and creates deliberate ambiguity about breakout timeline. We assess this is a calculated escalation lever rather than reactive measure.

warningIndicators.indicator: Direct IRGC ballistic missile strike on Israeli territory
warningIndicators.detail: First confirmed IRGC Shahab-3 strike on Haifa port district at 0218 UTC; 4 of 18 missiles penetrated defenses. Concurrent Iranian missiles observed over Tel Aviv with sirens and interceptor launches.
warningIndicators.assessment: Threshold breach — Iran has abandoned proxy-mediated retaliation model. We assess further direct strikes are probable; the resistance axis demonstrated coordinated multi-front timing.

warningIndicators.indicator: Houthi Red Sea attack resumption confirmed
warningIndicators.detail: USS Gravely intercepted 4 Houthi ASBMs; MV Nordic Hawk struck by UAV at Bab el-Mandeb with 3 crew wounded. Three-and-a-half month Houthi pause has ended.
warningIndicators.assessment: Red Sea attacks create dual-chokepoint risk — both Hormuz and Bab el-Mandeb now active threat zones. We assess war risk premiums will increase sharply for all Gulf and Red Sea routing.

warningIndicators.detail: CENTCOM confirms 14M bpd throughput vs normal 21M bpd (67%); daylight escort-only transit regime. VLCCs diverting to Cape route. P&I club cancellations remain in effect.
warningIndicators.assessment: Sustained throughput reduction at this level creates structural supply deficit that IEA releases can offset for approximately 30 days. We assess $140+ Brent is sustainable for conflict duration.

warningIndicators.indicator: IRGC cyber operations targeting Western critical infrastructure
warningIndicators.detail: CISA Advisory AA26-063A attributes active OT exploitation to IRGC-affiliated 'Volt Sparrow' targeting water and energy infrastructure. Unitronics PLC vulnerabilities exploited across North America and Europe.
warningIndicators.assessment: Direct relevance to Canadian critical infrastructure. Unitronics PLCs deployed in Canadian water/energy systems. We assess immediate defensive action warranted per CISA guidance.

warningIndicators.indicator: Iranian missile intercepted over NATO member territory
warningIndicators.detail: NATO air defenses intercepted an Iranian ballistic missile headed toward Turkey. Ankara confirmed missile was 'not intended for Turkiye' but was intercepted. Erdogan simultaneously condemned strikes as 'clear violation of international law.'
warningIndicators.assessment: Most significant Article 5-adjacent event this cycle. While the missile was assessed as errant rather than deliberately targeting Turkey, any future confirmed targeting of NATO member territory would trigger collective defense obligations with direct implications for Canada.

collectionGaps.gap: No visibility into current Fordow enrichment level or centrifuge cascade status post-IAEA suspension.
collectionGaps.significance: Nuclear breakout timeline assessment relies on pre-suspension baseline from ICG estimates; current enrichment rate and configuration unknown. This is the most significant intelligence gap this cycle.

collectionGaps.gap: No direct Lloyd's syndicate or broker quotes on post-March 5 premium levels; no JWC listed area update confirmed.
collectionGaps.significance: D6 assessment derived from operational indicators (CENTCOM escort data, UKMTO incidents, VLCC diversions) rather than direct market quotes. Premium hardening magnitude uncertain.

collectionGaps.gap: Unable to confirm attribution of Iranian internet disruption — government throttling vs external cyber operation vs infrastructure damage.
collectionGaps.significance: Affects assessment of US-Israeli cyber capability and Iranian regime intent; all three hypotheses remain viable. Recovery to 40% adds ambiguity.

collectionGaps.gap: Iranian leadership succession authority unclear following Khamenei assassination; funeral postponed under continued bombardment.
collectionGaps.significance: Unable to assess who has decision authority for ceasefire acceptance, nuclear escalation, or regime stability trajectory. No confirmed interlocutor for back-channel negotiations.

items.label: COLLECTION WINDOW
items.text: This cycle covers the 24-hour period ending 0600 UTC 07 March 2026.

items.label: NUCLEAR VISIBILITY
items.text: Assessment of Iranian nuclear breakout timeline relies on pre-suspension ICG estimates; IAEA monitoring discontinued at Fordow.

items.label: INSURANCE DATA
items.text: D6 assessment derived from operational indicators; direct broker quotes unavailable this cycle.

items.label: IRANIAN LEADERSHIP
items.text: Assessment of Iranian decision-making limited by succession uncertainty; Khamenei funeral postponed; no confirmed interlocutor.

caveats.confidenceAssessment: Overall assessment confidence is HIGH for D1, D2, D3 based on Tier 1 sourcing from CTP-ISW, CENTCOM, IAEA, UKMTO, Reuters, AP, Al Jazeera. D4 MODERATE due to reliance on Tier 2 diplomatic reporting and structurally deadlocked negotiating framework. D5 LOW due to Tier 3 cyber attribution reliance. D6 MODERATE — operational indicators from Tier 1 sources but no direct market quotes.

dissenterNotes.text: ANALYST B assesses that the Fordow suspension may be a bargaining chip rather than a prelude to breakout — Iran has used IAEA access as leverage in previous negotiations and the suspension coincides with demands for sanctions relief, suggesting Tehran is attempting to create negotiating pressure rather than pursuing weaponization. The 10–14 day breakout estimate from ICG assumes political decision has been made, which current evidence does not support.

caveats.sourceQuality: Tier 1 sources (CTP-ISW, CENTCOM, IAEA, UKMTO, Reuters, AP, Al Jazeera) provide factual floor across D1-D4. Tier 2 (BBC, NYT, Times of Israel, CFR Daily, ICG, CNBC Energy, Iran International) provide analytical depth. Tier 3 (NetBlocks, CISA, Recorded Future) used with appropriate caveats for D5. D6 relies on operational indicators from Tier 1 CENTCOM/UKMTO.
caveats.handling: PROTECTED B // TLP:AMBER — Not for public release
